summary: Plan is well-scoped, non-invasive, and follows established codebase patterns. Single new file plus a small integration point in the render loop.

risk_per_file.src/visual/cameraMotion.ts: low — self-contained module with no external side effects
risk_per_file.src/visual/renderLoop.ts: low — two-line integration (init + per-frame call) between existing evolveParams and renderer.render

pattern_consistency.harmonic_reuse: Matches evolution.ts layered sine harmonic pattern exactly
pattern_consistency.prng_reuse: Uses existing createPRNG from src/visual/prng.ts (note: plan says prng.ts, not seed/prng.ts — path is correct in codebase)
pattern_consistency.param_reuse: Leverages existing motionAmplitude (0.2 for reduced-motion, 1.0 otherwise) — no new params needed

integration_analysis.camera_current_state: Static at (0, 0, 5) — set in scene.ts, never modified in render loop
integration_analysis.insertion_point: After evolveParams() (line 143) and before renderer.render() (line 200) in renderLoop.ts
integration_analysis.pointer_isolation: Pointer entropy feeds only geometry disturbance via mappings.ts — no camera coupling exists

acceptance_criteria_coverage.parallax_across_depth: covered — 3-axis incommensurate drift ensures diagonal paths revealing depth layers
acceptance_criteria_coverage.smooth_and_seeded: covered — sine harmonics are C∞ smooth; all parameters derived from createPRNG(seed + ':camera')
acceptance_criteria_coverage.pointer_does_not_control_orbit: covered — cameraMotion.ts has no pointer input; pointer path is architecturally separate

suggestions: Import path for createPRNG should be '../visual/prng' (not '../seed/prng') — verify at implementation time
Consider clamping total displacement magnitude as noted in risks — a simple vec3 length check suffices
